title: README quality pass — match the CodeYam showcase bar
userRequest:
## Summary Raise Otterpace's public GitHub `README.md` to the quality bar set by the two sibling CodeYam showcase repos — [codeyam-ai/codeyam-counter](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/codeyam-counter) and [codeyam-ai/tabcommand](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/tabcommand). Those READMEs share a tight, scannable shape: a badge row, a bold one-line tagline, a short description, a centered hero screenshot, an availability/install note, a copy-pasteable "Develop with codeyam-editor" block (clone → install editor → launch), a local build/test block, the auto-managed scenario gallery, and Contributing + License footers. Otterpace's current README is richer in prose but is missing the badge row, the hero image, the clone/install steps in the run-and-edit block, and the Contributing/License footers — and it never states that Otterpace itself is one of these CodeYam showcase apps alongside Counter and TabCommand. This plan restructures the README to match that bar while keeping Otterpace's genuinely valuable deep-dive content (the "Built with CodeYam" narrative, architecture, testing notes), trimming where the references stay lean. ## Key Decisions - **License badge only, no CI badge** — the reference READMEs open with a CI + License badge row, but Otterpace has no `.github/workflows/` CI, so a CI badge would 404. Add only the real MIT License badge (`[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](./LICENSE)`). Do NOT add a CI badge and do NOT create a CI workflow in this plan. - **TestFlight / coming-soon availability note, not a dead App Store link** — Counter and TabCommand both have a store-availability section; Otterpace is submitted to the App Store (2026-07-10) but not yet live, so add a short availability line framed as "in TestFlight / App Store review" with a clearly marked placeholder to swap for the live App Store link once it ships. Never publish a dead store link. - **Reference otterpace.com** — the app has a live marketing site at [otterpace.com](https://otterpace.com). Add it to the opening description and the Availability section (and optionally as a website badge/link), so a visitor has a canonical product link even before the App Store listing is live. - **Add a centered hero screenshot** using the existing `today-goal-crushed--iphone-16.png` capture, mirroring the references' `

` pattern (iPhone portrait → ~300px). - **Explicitly position Otterpace as a CodeYam showcase app** in the opening and the "Built with CodeYam" section, naming its siblings (Counter, TabCommand) so a visitor understands it was built with, and is a worked example of, codeyam-editor. - **Preserve, don't discard, the deep content** — keep the "Built with CodeYam", Architecture, Running, and Testing sections (they're a strength over the leaner references), but tighten the top of the file so the first screenful is as scannable as the references. Deep prose moves below the fold. - **Keep the auto-managed marker blocks intact** — the `` and `` regions are regenerated by the editor tooling. Only edit content inside them to match the references' clone/ install steps; never remove or rename the markers. ## Implementation ### 1. Add a badge row + tagline under the title **File**: `README.md` Directly under the `# Otterpace 🐾` heading, add a License badge and keep a bold one-line tagline (the references lead with a bold sentence): ```markdown # Otterpace 🐾 [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](./LICENSE) **An open-source, AI running coach for iOS — built in the open as a CodeYam showcase.** ``` Keep the existing friendly description paragraph that follows. Add one sentence naming the sibling showcase apps, e.g. "Otterpace is one of the CodeYam showcase apps, alongside [CodeYam Counter](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/codeyam-counter) and [TabCommand](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/tabcommand)." ### 2. Add a centered hero screenshot **File**: `README.md` Immediately after the description paragraph, add a centered hero image using the existing goal-crushed capture (matches the references' hero placement and width): ```markdown

``` ### 3. Add an availability / TestFlight note **File**: `README.md` Replace the current prose "## Launch" section (which is maintainer-facing go-live runbook pointer material) with a short, user-facing availability note near the top, and move the go-live runbook links down into a maintainer-facing subsection (or under Running). New availability block: ```markdown ## Availability Otterpace is in **TestFlight / App Store review** — learn more at [otterpace.com](https://otterpace.com). A public App Store link will land here once it's live. ``` Also weave [otterpace.com](https://otterpace.com) into the opening description paragraph as the canonical product link. Keep the go-live-runbook + area deep-dive links, but relocate them under a maintainer-facing heading so the top of the README reads as product-facing (like the references) rather than launch-ops-facing. ### 4. Bring the run-and-edit block in line with the references **File**: `README.md` (inside the `` markers) The references' block includes clone → install editor → launch. Update Otterpace's block to the same three steps (keeping its real repo URL and, since it's a Swift project, no `npm install` for the app itself): ```bash # Clone the repo git clone https://github.com/codeyam-ai/otterpace && cd otterpace # Install codeyam-editor npm install -g @codeyam-editor/codeyam-editor@latest # Launch the editor (split-screen terminal + live preview) codeyam-editor editor ``` Do not remove the marker comments. Preserve the existing swift-test command line if the tooling keeps it inside the block. ### 5. Add Contributing + License footers **File**: `README.md` The repo already ships `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `SECURITY.md`, and `LICENSE`, but the README never links them. Add footer sections mirroring Counter's: ```markdown ## Contributing Contributions are welcome! Please read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for build/test instructions and the PR process, and note our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). To report a security issue, see [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md). ## License [MIT](./LICENSE) © 2026 Otterpace ``` (LICENSE header reads "Copyright (c) 2026 Otterpace", so attribute to Otterpace.) ### 6. Tighten the top-of-file ordering and reinforce the CodeYam framing **File**: `README.md` Reorder so the first screenful matches the references' flow: title → badge → tagline → description (+ sibling-apps line) → hero image → Availability → a brief "What's here today" teaser. Keep the detailed "What's here today", "Built with CodeYam", "Architecture", "Running", "App icon & launch screen", and "Testing" sections below, in that order. In "Built with CodeYam", add one line explicitly tying Otterpace to the showcase family and linking codeyam.com, so the "how it was made" story is unmistakable. Leave the scenario-gallery marker block where the tooling manages it. ## Reused existing code This is a documentation-only change; no source code is reused, but it links and depends on existing repo assets: - `LICENSE` (MIT, © 2026 Otterpace) — badge target + License footer - `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `SECURITY.md` — Contributing footer links - `.codeyam/scenarios/screenshots/today-goal-crushed--iphone-16.png` — hero image - `docs/go-live-runbook.md` and area deep-dives — retained, relocated to a maintainer subsection - [otterpace.com](https://otterpace.com) — canonical product/marketing site, linked from the description + Availability - Follow-up: [readme-app-store-link-swap](.codeyam/plans/readme-app-store-link-swap.md) — the post-launch link update this plan leaves TODO placeholders for - Existing `` and `` marker blocks — edited in place, markers preserved - Reference models: [codeyam-ai/codeyam-counter](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/codeyam-counter) and [codeyam-ai/tabcommand](https://github.com/codeyam-ai/tabcommand) READMEs ## Scenarios to Demonstrate This is a README/documentation change with no runtime UI surface, so there are no new app scenarios to capture. Verification is by rendered-markdown review: - README renders on GitHub with a working License badge (no broken CI badge) - Hero screenshot resolves and displays centered at ~300px - Availability note shows the TestFlight/coming-soon framing with the TODO placeholder - Contributing + License footer links resolve to the in-repo files - The `codeyam:run-and-edit` and `codeyam:scenario-gallery` marker blocks are still present and intact - First screenful is as scannable as the Counter / TabCommand references while the deep-dive sections remain below the fold
description: Restructured the public README.md to the quality bar set by the sibling CodeYam showcase repos (codeyam-counter, tabcommand): added an MIT license badge, a bold tagline, a centered iPhone hero screenshot (today-goal-crushed), a live App Store availability line plus otterpace.com link, a copy-pasteable clone -> install-editor -> launch block, and Contributing/License footers. Otterpace is now explicitly positioned as a CodeYam showcase app alongside its siblings, while the deeper Built-with-CodeYam, Architecture, Running, and Testing sections were preserved and moved below the fold so the first screenful stays scannable. Docs-only change: no app source, components, or scenarios were modified, and the codeyam-managed run-and-edit and scenario-gallery marker blocks were left intact.
